summary: Ribosome detachment is characteristic of permanent rather than transient disruption; other listed options represent minor alterations allowing potential return to normal function if stressors cease. The critical distinction lies between recoverability through reversibility or irreversibly compromising essential functions like protein synthesis via ribosomes. Reversible injuries include ER swelling, mitochondrial modifications, and membrane blebs which can be restored with appropriate conditions whereas loss of functional organelles signals terminal stages leading away from full regeneration capabilities. Agent2 concludes by emphasizing how ribosomal detachment signifies an endpoint beyond repair due its integral role within cells' ability to survive under adverse circumstances compared to milder forms
